title: MMC0 reset binding diagnostic test
question: Does replacing the raw vendor SDMMC0 reset cell 35 with the current Linux binding RST_BUS_MMC0 clear the A733 SDMMC0 IDMAC descriptor-read stall?
test_cost: medium; one DTS-only patch, one DTB build, one Cubie3 UART boot

inputs.kernel_commit: 1d637f477818 arm64: dts: test A733 MMC0 reset binding

change.from: resets = <&ccu 35>; /* diag: vendor SDMMC0 reset cell 0x23 */

observations.boot: Linux 7.1.0-rc5-00283-g86785e682c32 booted on Radxa Cubie A7S.
observations.card: mmcblk0 appeared before the forced CMD18 diagnostic.
observations.before_cmdr: desc ring before-cmdr: d0={8000003c,00001000,40575000,3f840004}, chda=0, cbda=0, idst=0.
observations.after_cmdr: idma-post-cmdr: IDST=0x00004000, CHDA=DLBA 0x3f840000, CBDA=0, CBCR=0x400, BBCR=0.
observations.after_cpu_sync: descriptor checksum and descriptor word 0 remained unchanged after CPU sync; OWN stayed set.

decision.summary: Using the current Linux binding reset cell RST_BUS_MMC0 does not clear the descriptor-read stall. The raw reset-cell mismatch is a cleanup issue for the diagnostic tree, but not the root cause of IDMAC failing to fetch descriptor word 0.
decision.next_step: Keep the diagnostic tree committed for reproducibility, but do not submit this as an upstream fix. Upstream DTS should eventually use the accepted reset binding symbol, not raw cells.

restore.cubie3: verified vendor kernel 5.15.147-21-a733 after power cycle
